game_info.title: Crumble Chaos
game_info.concept:
Crumble is a dynamic physics platformer. Move like a Slime, jump like a ball, swing like Spiderman! Steam URL: https://store.steampowered.com/app/1061180/Crumble/ --game_index 55511 --config configs/generators/threejs_gen.yaml --model google:gemini-3-pro-preview ================================================================================ Full Description: ================================================================================ Crumble is a dynamic physics platformer. Move like a Slime, jump like a ball, swing like Spiderman! Wishlist and follow the development of my upcoming game:


Crumble is a physics platformer with a grappling tongue mechanic.
Join the chaos of unstable platforms and unexpected destructions with this cute blue ball!
Try to progress in exciting levels falling around you.
Move like a Slime, jump like a Ball, swing like Spiderman!

Features

  • A campaign mode with an increasingly crumbling world
  • A multiplayer Party Mode up to 4 local players
  • Unlock new skins
  • Online leader boards
  • High dynamic physics

Vibrant Environment

Crumbling Chaos

Swing Mechanics
game_info.description: Crumble Chaos is a physics-based 3D platformer where you control an unstable blue slime-ball. Roll, jump, and grapple your way through a world that literally falls apart beneath you. Use your sticky tongue to swing across vast gaps, bounce off surfaces, and race against the destruction of the environment. Reach the finish line before the ground disappears!
game_info.controls:
- Arrow Keys / WASD: Roll and move the ball
- SPACE: Jump (Hold for higher jump)
- Z or Left Shift: Shoot Grappling Tongue / Swing (Hold to stay attached)
- R: Restart Game
- ESC: Pause / Resume
- ENTER: Start Game

TEST_1.strategy_description: Simulate holding the Forward key (W) for 60 frames. Monitor the player's Z position to ensure it changes significantly in the negative Z direction (forward in 3D space).
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The player's Z position should decrease by at least 5 units, indicating successful movement physics and velocity application.

TEST_2.strategy_description: Simulate pressing the SPACE key while the player is grounded. Monitor the player's vertical velocity and Y position.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The player's Y position should increase above the ground level, and vertical velocity should become positive initially, then decrease due to gravity.
TEST_2.strategy: Simulate pressing the SPACE key while the player is grounded. Monitor the player's vertical velocity and Y position.
TEST_2.expected: The player's Y position should increase above the ground level, and vertical velocity should become positive initially, then decrease due to gravity.

TEST_3.strategy_description: Teleport the player onto a "CrumblePlatform". Wait for the platform's trigger duration (e.g., 1 second). Observe the platform's state and physics properties.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The platform should transition from 'STABLE' to 'FALLING' state. Its Y position should begin to decrease as gravity is applied to it.
TEST_3.strategy: Teleport the player onto a "CrumblePlatform". Wait for the platform's trigger duration (e.g., 1 second). Observe the platform's state and physics properties.
TEST_3.expected: The platform should transition from 'STABLE' to 'FALLING' state. Its Y position should begin to decrease as gravity is applied to it.

TEST_4.test_description: Verify grappling hook attachment and swinging physics.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Position player near a grapple point over a gap. Simulate holding the Grapple key (Z). Check if 'isGrappling' becomes true and if a constraint force is applied preventing the player from falling straight down.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Player state 'isGrappling' should be true. The player should swing in an arc rather than falling vertically into the void.

TEST_7.strategy_description: Trigger a game over, then simulate pressing 'R'. Check if player position is reset to start and phase is back to START.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Player position should be (0, 2, 0), score should be 0, and gamePhase should be "START".
